directions.observed: both pupils and nose lift above the neutral head center
directions.reason: clear upward eye and muzzle pitch

directions.observed: eyes remain high with a small screen-right nose shift
directions.reason: first even step from up toward right

directions.observed: pupils and muzzle move farther screen-right while remaining elevated
strong screen-right head yaw with a remaining upward eye cue
directions.reason: continuous approach to the right cardinal

directions.observed: nose and visible pupil sit unmistakably screen-right of head center
directions.reason: matches the approved right cardinal family

directions.observed: rightward yaw is retained as the eyes begin moving down
directions.reason: first even step below the right cardinal

directions.observed: nose and pupils remain screen-right with a clearer downward head pitch
head and eyes point mostly down while retaining a screen-right cue
directions.reason: lands one continuous step before down

continuity.evidence: Paws, lower torso, baseline, body scale, tail attachment, face identity, and anatomical markings remain stable; adjacent head and eye motion advances without reversal, snap, crop, or scale pop.
